title: Will Marco Rubio visit Iran before Jul 1, 2026?

current.lean_note: ADVERSARIAL VETO: The position is overconfident and lacks a credible edge over the market, which has already priced in a much lower probability. The reasoning is speculative and does not justify the proposed probability.
adversarial_concerns: The proposed probability (0.12) is vastly higher than the market-implied probability (0.002), indicating a significant divergence that lacks justification.
The reasoning does not provide sufficient evidence to support a probability that is over 50 times higher than the market's implied probability.
The forecaster assumes Rubio's involvement in discussions around the Iran deal implies a likelihood of him visiting Iran, but this is a logical leap without evidence of intent or opportunity.
current.rationale_summary: The base rate indicates that U.S.-Iran diplomatic efforts have historically faced significant challenges due to mutual distrust and regional tensions. Recent evidence suggests that U.S.-Iran peace talks are in question due to recent Gulf attacks, and Gulf allies have concerns about any Iran deal, which may hinder diplomatic progress. While Rubio is involved in discussions around the Iran deal, the
key_drivers: Recent Gulf attacks complicating peace talks
Gulf allies' concerns about Iran deals
No direct evidence of Rubio visiting Iran
reference_classes: Historical Senate member foreign policy engagement

history.lean_note: I lean NO (my prob 1%) but it isn't +EV at the ask — no value bet (betting the other side would contradict my forecast).
history.rationale_summary: All US-Iran negotiations occur in third countries (Oman, Pakistan, Geneva), the prospective signing is in Geneva with VP Vance not Rubio, and no US Secretary of State has visited Iran since 1979 — a Rubio visit to Tehran before July 1 is virtually impossible.
reference_classes: 0 US SecState visits to Iran in 47 years

history.lean_note: I lean NO (my prob 1%) but it isn't +EV at the ask — no value bet (betting the other side would contradict my forecast).
history.rationale_summary: No US Secretary of State has visited Iran since 1979; 2026 talks held in neutral venues (Geneva); no plan to travel to Tehran.
reference_classes: US officials physically visiting Iran post-1979

history.lean_note: no +EV value bet at the ask (my prob 1%, modal NO) — staying out.
history.rationale_summary: Rubio is on a confirmed Gulf tour (UAE/Kuwait/Bahrain, Jun 23-25) with Iran not on the itinerary; no sitting SecState has entered Iran since 1979.
reference_classes: US SecState visits to Iran since 1979 = zero
